exemplar.title: Do the Profit and Sustainability Rules in the Premier League restrict promoted clubs from sustainably increasing revenues?

detailedExemplarData.title: Do the Profit and Sustainability Rules in the Premier League restrict promoted clubs from sustainably increasing revenues?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear, precise statement of topic and comprehensive contextual background.
Research question is sharply focused and consistently linked to the discussion.
Methodology employs an appropriate mix of quantitative and qualitative sources with evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: Lacks brief roadmap of subsequent sections in the introduction.
Does not signal sample size limitations early enough.
Specific dataâanalysis techniques (e.g., ratio formulae) are not fully detailed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates the topic accurately and provides comprehensive explanation, formulates a clear, well-focused research question, and uses a varied, appropriately justified methodology. Minor omissions (section roadmap, early limitation signalling, explicit dataâanalysis detail) prevent a perfect 6, but overall the criterion is met at a high level.

strengths: Demonstrates clear understanding of football finance concepts and regulatory mechanisms.
Applies mostly relevant and appropriate source material to support financial arguments.
Uses terminology and concepts accurately and consistently throughout.
weaknesses: Occasional descriptive rather than analytical deployment of business theories.
Some source applications are peripheral to the core research question.
Stretched or under-evidenced theoretical examples (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces linkages).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows clear subject knowledge and consistently accurate use of terminology, supported by generally relevant source material. However, theoretical applications sometimes lapse into description or tangential discussion, and some source uses lack full integration into the central argument, placing this criterion solidly in the mid-band.

strengths: Analysis is clear, structured, and supported by quantitative and qualitative evidence.
Arguments are well-developed with balanced evaluation across case studies.
Research draws on a good breadth of relevant primary and secondary sources.
weaknesses: Analytical depth is limited to headline figures without deeper cash-flow or benchmarking analysis.
Some passages remain descriptive rather than critically probing counter-evidence.
Peripheral examples occasionally dilute the focus on promoted clubs.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates solid critical thinking: clear, well-supported analysis and a coherent, balanced evaluation of evidence. Research quality is good and consistently relevant. However, deeper quantitative interrogation and more rigorous critical evaluation in places would be required for higher marks in this criterion.

strengths: Structure fully meets the requirements of an extended essay with logical sectioning and smooth transitions.
Layout includes all essential elements (title page, contents, page numbering, bibliography) in a clear, reader-friendly format.
Formatting complies with IB guidelines, enhancing readability.
weaknesses: Some lengthy paragraphs and blocks of text impede flow and could be broken into sub-sections.
Heading levels are occasionally inconsistent, and long URLs in the bibliography affect polish.
Minor spacing inconsistencies around headings slightly disrupt visual uniformity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is strong and fully meets the highest band descriptors: the report is well-structured, navigable, and formatted correctly. Only minor layout and consistency issues prevent further refinement.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Research question is sharp and consistently foregrounded.** It is phrased as: âDo the Profit and Sustainability Rules in the Premier League restrict promoted clubs from sustainably increasing revenues?â â a clear, focused, cause-and-effect enquiry.

Throughout the essay the student returns to this question when analysing each case study and in the conclusion, showing an **effective linkage between the RQ and discussion**. The choice of three recent promoted clubs gives an operational focus that is neither too broad nor too narrow for the word limit.

Some drifting into wider issues (e.g., VAR, CSR controversies) occasionally dilutes the laser focus on ârevenue restrictionâ, preventing a perfect score, but the overall formulation and connection to analysis justify a strong 5-6 band rating; a 5 is awarded.

**Well-structured discussion culminating in balanced evaluation.** The student weighs differing club approaches, acknowledges methodological limitations, and recognises that PSR constraints vary with managerial strategy rather than being uniformly restrictive.

Evaluation is explicitly signposted in each case study (e.g., identifying Brentford as evidence against the hypothesis, Forest as supporting evidence) and synthesized in a reflective conclusion that suggests avenues for future research.

Critical depth could be richerâsome assertions about causality and theoretical fit are accepted without probing counter-evidenceâbut the argument remains coherent and reasoned, meeting the 7-9 band descriptors; a score of 7 is justified.

**Demonstrates clear understanding of football finance and business management concepts.** The student explains PSR mechanics, amortisation, ratio analysis and broadcasting revenue structures with accuracy and contextual relevance.

Business theories (SWOT, Lewinâs Change Model, Porterâs Five Forces/Generic Strategies, Kaizen, BCG matrix) are invoked to interpret club strategies, showing breadth of subject knowledge. Nevertheless, explanations occasionally lapse into description rather than analytical deployment, and some theoretical applications (e.g., calling player substitutions a Porter substitution force) are stretched.

The coherence and generally correct use of sources place the work in the 3-4 band; a score of 4 recognises strong but not wholly authoritative mastery.

**Terminology is accurate, varied and consistently applied.** Key financial terms (amortisation, net profit margin, wage-to-revenue ratio), regulatory vocabulary (PSR limits, three-year loss window) and management concepts (Kaizen, Lewinâs model, AIDA) are used correctly throughout.

The student integrates these concepts naturally into the narrative rather than listing jargon, indicating secure command of subject language. Occasional minor slips (e.g., plural agreement, stylistic issues) do not undermine clarity or correctness.

Given the sustained, accurate deployment of specialised terminology across sections, the work meets the criteria for the 5-6 band; a score of 5 is appropriate.

**Clear and often insightful analysis.** The essay dissects each clubâs financial data, links ratios and loss limits to strategic choices, and employs business models to explore causes and consequences.

Conclusions (e.g., Brentfordâs data-led prudence vs. Forestâs overspending) are generally well supported by evidence. The use of frameworks (SWOT, BCG, Porter) helps structure reasoning and demonstrates multi-angle consideration.

Nonetheless, some analytical passages remain descriptive (e.g., technology discussion) and the depth of quantitative analysis is limited to headline figures without deeper cash-flow or comparative benchmarking. This aligns with the âclear analysis with well-supported conclusionsâ description; hence a score of 7 within the 7-9 band.

**Mostly relevant, well-chosen material.** Annual reports, Deloitte finance reviews and reputable journalism underpin the discussion, and numerical data (revenues, wage bills, PSR limits) are regularly cited to support points.

Application is generally sound: figures are linked to arguments about compliance, competitive pressure and revenue growth, and tables/diagrams such as the BCG matrix are used to illustrate thinking. However, some references (e.g., Tottenhamâs Korean fan base) are tangential to the RQ, and certain assertions (e.g., kit sales statistics) are presented without direct integration into the core analysis of PSR restrictions.

Because relevance and linkage are "mostly" but not always fully aligned, the strand fits the 3-4 band; the upper mark of 4 is merited for consistently appropriate though occasionally peripheral source use.

**Appropriate and varied methods and sources.** The student combines quantitative (club financial statements, transfer spending, ratio analysis) and qualitative (theoretical frameworks, media reports) data, demonstrating conscious methodological planning.

Evidence of **informed selection** is clear: primary sources (Companies House accounts) are triangulated with authoritative secondary sources (Deloitte, Human Rights Watch, The Athletic). The rationale for choosing Brentford, Fulham and Nottingham Forest is explained, highlighting diversity of experiences.

Limitations (small sample, hidden transactions) are acknowledged, yet the methodology could have specified data-handling techniques (e.g., how ratios were calculated) more rigorously. Overall the range and justification align with the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects solid but not exemplary execution.

**Good breadth and relevance of research.** Primary financial statements, industry reports and credible media sources furnish up-to-date quantitative and qualitative evidence directly tied to the RQ.

The student shows clear linkage between data and inquiry, selecting three contrasting case studies that collectively illuminate different PSR responses. Citations are frequent and typically appropriate, indicating thorough engagement with available material.

Research could be deepened via interviews with club officials or academic literature on sports finance, and some peripheral examples dilute focus. These factors keep the strand within, but at the lower end of, the 7-9 band; a score of 7 reflects solid, clearly relevant research.

**Layout is largely compliant with IB guidelines.** Word count, title, candidate code and page numbering are supplied; the contents table is clear; font size, headings and spacing are reader-friendly. The bibliography follows a recognisable citation style, and in-text referencing is generally consistent.

The inclusion of a colour graphic is appropriate and labelled. Minor inconsistencies in link formatting and occasional long URLs slightly affect polish, but overall formatting supports readability and academic convention.

Given the correct application of required elements with only negligible lapses, the work earns the upper mark of the 3-4 band; hence a score of 4.

**Clear, precise statement of topic and scope.** The student introduces the Premier Leagueâs Profit and Sustainability Rules (PSR) and immediately frames the essay around their effect on newly-promoted clubsâ ability to grow revenue, making the field and focus explicit.

The introduction provides **detailed contextual background** (history of PSR, rationale for the rules, relevance to promoted clubs) and explains why the issue matters for competitive balance and financial health. Key terms such as "sustainably increasing revenues" are defined in practical terms (e.g., long-term viability, compliance limits).

Minor limitations include the absence of a brief outline of subsequent sections and the small sample size not being signalled as a potential limitation at this early stage. Nevertheless, the communication is accurate, purposeful and comprehensive, justifying placement in the 5-6 band; a score of 5 recognises the very goodâbut not flawlessâclarity and completeness.

**Meets structural expectations of an Extended Essay.** Title page, abstract-style introduction, contents page with page references, logical sectioning (Introduction, Methodology, Main Body, Conclusion, Bibliography) are present and functional.

Transitions between sections are generally smooth, and sub-headings for each case study aid navigability. Minor issues such as occasional long blocks of text and an isolated embedded image that disrupts flow do not materially impair the structure.

All core elements are in place and appropriately ordered, fulfilling the top-band requirement for structure; a score of 4 within the 3-4 band is awarded.

annotations.comment: The introduction provides rich historical and regulatory context on PSR, effectively situating the research question within the broader landscape of football finance.
The rationale for selecting this sample of seasons and clubs is noted but lacks depth; clarify how these criteria ensure representativeness and address potential sampling bias.
The aim statement reiterates the topic broadly. Consider integrating this with the research questionâs causeâeffect focus to avoid redundancy and sharpen the opening.
An HTML <code> tag appears within a URL, disrupting readability. Remove code elements and display clean hyperlinks or reference labels instead.

The research question is articulated with precision and scope, clearly framing the enquiry into PSRâs impact on promoted clubsâ revenue growth. This focus establishes a strong foundation for the investigation.
The detailed contents page with clear section titles and accurate page references enhances navigability and overall presentation.
Your innovative use of the BCG matrix for player assets is promising, but you should define the quantitative thresholds used to classify each quadrant.
This dense block of incentives analysis would be more readerâfriendly if broken into subâheadings or bullet points to guide the audience.
The Tottenham example is interesting but drifts from your core focus on promoted clubs; tighten the link or consider replacing with a caseâspecific illustration.
Brand equity discussion would be strengthened by including data on merchandise or sponsorship growth specific to the promoted clubs under study.
This lengthy paragraph would benefit from a clear topic sentence and division into distinct analytical points to improve structural flow.
Insert a transitional sentence before the âBrentfordâ heading to guide the reader smoothly into the first case study.
Porterâs Five Forces is invoked without explicitly linking each force to Fulhamâs financial data; ensure theoretical concepts tie directly to numerical evidence.
The application of Kaizen to Fulhamâs incremental strategic adjustments demonstrates insightful multi-angle analysis.
The summary of Fulhamâs SWOT lacks visual support; consider adding a table or diagram to present strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats more clearly.
The inclusion of a precise word count (3987) at the start demonstrates compliance with IB formatting requirements.
The paragraph on technological advantages is descriptive. Deepen your evaluation by analysing how PSR restricts investment in these technologies for smaller clubs.
The CSR scrutiny example (Newcastle) is compelling but needs clearer integration into your analysis of promoted clubsâ revenue trajectories under PSR.
The levelâ3 heading for âBrentfordâ breaks the earlier hierarchy. Ensure consistent use of heading levels throughout to maintain clarity.
The Brentford case introduction clearly summarises strategic key points and sets up its relevance to PSR, demonstrating focused method.
The application of Lewinâs Change Model is appropriate but would be stronger with citation of the original source and a quantitative link to financial transition metrics.
The detailed ratio analysis with specific year-on-year revenue and profit figures clearly supports your conclusion on PSR compliance.
The description of PSR loss allowances would benefit from citation of the original Premier League regulatory document to ensure accuracy and strengthen source credibility.
The mixed-methods design, combining quantitative and qualitative sources with clear case-study justification, demonstrates informed methodological planning.
Outline the specific dataâanalysis techniques (e.g., formulae for ratio calculations) to enhance transparency of your quantitative methodology.
The term âsecondary sourcesâ is too general; specify which journals or reports inform your qualitative analysis to clarify source relevance.
Segmenting the analysis into PSR compliance, financial decisionâmaking, and revenue impact provides a coherent methodological framework for case evaluation.
The limitations section notes sample size but omits discussion of potential mediaâsource bias; deepening this reflection would strengthen critical awareness.
The marketing mix discussion remains at a descriptive level; critically assess how PSR constraints may limit matchday revenue enhancements for promoted clubs.
Framing Fulham as a âyo-yoâ club effectively illustrates the cyclical pressure of promotion and relegation on revenue and PSR compliance.
The Break-Even principle is mentioned but would benefit from a formal equation (e.g., $$	ext{BreakEvenSales}= rac{	ext{FixedCosts}}{	ext{ContributionMargin}}$$) to enhance analytical rigor.
The Nottingham Forest case effectively highlights the misalignment between aggressive spending and PSR limits, underscoring critical evaluation.
There is a minor typo in Forestâs revenue figure (â154. 8â); correct to âÂ£154.8 millionâ to maintain numerical precision.
The customized BCG matrix vividly conveys player categories, reinforcing your theoretical application to Nottinghamâs recruitment decisions.
The âQuestion Markâ category discussion could be enhanced by providing average transfer fees or performance metrics for those signings.
The critique of Forestâs branding and sponsorship deals is general; include specific examples of negotiations or failed partnerships for deeper evaluation.
Adjust spacing above the Conclusion heading to match preceding section layouts, ensuring consistent formatting and readability.
The conclusion synthesizes comparative case insights effectively, clearly answering the research question and reflecting on strategic implications.
Brand Equity Theory is mentioned but would be more compelling if supported by metrics such as sponsorship values or social-media engagement figures.
The future research recommendations are broad; specify potential methodologies (e.g., interviews, regression analysis) to guide further study.
The reflection on research limitations and the call for broader qualitative factors demonstrates evaluative engagement and awareness of the studyâs boundaries.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent line breaks and long URLs inâtext; adopt a standard citation style (e.g., APA or Harvard) for uniformity.
The reference list mixes formatting styles and embeds long access URLs; consolidate to a single, consistent citation format to enhance professionalism.
